name: RP6 — PS2 / GameCube Sustained
description: Retroid Pocket 6 / AYN Thor (SD8Gen2 / kalama, 3-cluster). Caps little (policy0) to 1555 MHz, gold (policy3) to 1920 MHz, prime (policy7) to 2227 MHz — all one or two OPP steps below the thermal throttle trigger on this SoC. GPU ~70% (~476 MHz of 680 MHz max). Governor walt. This is the sustained-performance sweet spot: capping below the throttle trigger means the SoC holds its clocks indefinitely instead of boosting to 3187 MHz for 8 seconds then dropping to 1.2 GHz. Tested on RP6 by Calibrate-SoC community. Does not persist across reboot.

name: RP6 — Light Emulation (N64 / PSP / Dreamcast)
description: Retroid Pocket 6 / AYN Thor (SD8Gen2 / kalama, 3-cluster). Caps little (policy0) to 1228 MHz, gold (policy3) to 1536 MHz, prime (policy7) to 1363 MHz — roughly 60-65% of each cluster's ceiling. GPU ~60% (~407 MHz of 680 MHz). Governor schedutil. Designed for 5th/6th-gen emulators that need moderate single-thread performance but not the prime cluster at full tilt. Fan stays quiet in most environments. Does not persist across reboot.

name: RP6 — Cool & Quiet / Max Battery
description: Retroid Pocket 6 / AYN Thor (SD8Gen2 / kalama, 3-cluster). Caps little (policy0) to 902 MHz (~45%), gold (policy3) to 844 MHz (~30%), prime (policy7) to 998 MHz (~31%). GPU ~45% (~306 MHz of 680 MHz). Governor conservative — scales up on real load, defaults to low clocks so the fan is silent and battery lasts ~2x longer. Best for menus, 2D retro, video streaming. NOT suitable for 3D emulation. Does not persist across reboot.

name: Odin 3 — Anti-Throttle / Small (TheOldTaylor)
description: AYN Odin 3 (Snapdragon 8 Elite / SM8750, 2-cluster: policy0 6 cores max 3532.8 MHz, policy6 2 prime cores max 4320 MHz). TheOldTaylor's mildest underclock: policy0 → 2745.6 MHz (~78% of 3532.8), policy6 → 3072 MHz (~71% of 4320). This is the highest clock that remains thermally sustainable for long gaming sessions on the Odin 3, measured and reported by TheOldTaylor. Small thermal/power benefit with almost no performance loss vs stock. Three perfd daemons must be stopped and scaling_max_freq chmod-locked around writes (handled by the app). Does not persist across reboot.

name: Odin 3 — PS2 / GC Sustained / Large (TheOldTaylor)
description: AYN Odin 3 (SD8 Elite, 2-cluster). TheOldTaylor's aggressive cap: policy0 → 1785.6 MHz (~51% of 3532.8), policy6 → 1958.4 MHz (~45% of 4320). At this cap ~14W from ~20W in 1080p AAA titles, temps 50-60°C from 90-100°C. Excellent for PS2/GC/Wii emulation where sustained 60fps beats burst-then-throttle. Fan noticeably quieter. Three perfd daemons stopped + chmod-lock required. Does not persist across reboot.

name: Odin 3 — Switch / Medium (TheOldTaylor)
description: AYN Odin 3 (SD8 Elite, 2-cluster). TheOldTaylor's middle preset: policy0 → 2227.2 MHz (~63% of 3532.8), policy6 → 2246.4 MHz (~52% of 4320). Balances headroom for Switch emulation against sustained thermal stability. Less power savings than Large but more headroom for demanding AAA titles. Three perfd daemons stopped + chmod-lock required. Does not persist across reboot.
